scope: Seed state-level security deposit and prepaid rent rules for V3 risk flagging.
usage_note: Use for uncertainty-aware pre-application risk output. Do not present as legal advice.

rules.cap_formula: Most residential security deposits are capped at one month's rent under AB 12 / Civil Code 1950.5, with limited small-landlord exceptions.
rules.small_landlord_exception: Some small landlords may collect up to two months' rent under statutory exception conditions.
rules.furnished_exception: Prior furnished/unfurnished distinction was changed by AB 12 for most landlords.
rules.product_risk_note: If upfront deposit-like charges exceed one month's rent in CA, flag for exception review before applying.

rules.cap_formula: Deposit or advance may not exceed one month's rent.
rules.prepaid_rent_limit: Advance payments are included in the one-month limit.
rules.source_citation: New York General Obligations Law section 7-108
rules.product_risk_note: If NY move-in asks for more than first month plus one-month deposit, flag as high ask-before-paying risk.

rules.cap_formula: At or before tenancy commencement, listed allowed charges include first month, last month, one-month security deposit, and lock/key purchase and installation cost.
rules.prepaid_rent_limit: Last month's rent can be charged in addition to first month and security deposit.
rules.product_risk_note: MA fee stacks are high-friction; separate first, last, security, lock/key, and broker fee before giving a verdict.

rules.cap_formula: Seed source governs security deposit definition and return duties, but does not set a statewide deposit amount cap in this dataset.
rules.source_citation: Texas State Law Library security deposit guide / Texas Property Code Chapter 92
rules.product_risk_note: Do not claim a TX deposit cap from this seed data. Ask whether the deposit and application deposit are separate.

rules.cap_formula: Landlord may not demand or receive security, however denominated, including prepaid rent, of more than one and one-half months' rent; tenant may voluntarily pay more in advance.
rules.prepaid_rent_limit: Included in the 1.5x cap unless voluntarily paid in advance.
rules.product_risk_note: If AZ required upfront security plus prepaid rent exceeds 1.5x rent, flag for review.

rules.cap_formula: Two weeks' rent for week-to-week, 1.5 months for month-to-month, and two months for terms greater than month-to-month.
rules.product_risk_note: NC result should ask lease term before applying the cap.

rules.cap_formula: Seed source governs deposit/advance-rent handling, notice, and return duties, but this dataset does not assert a statewide amount cap.
rules.product_risk_note: Do not claim a FL deposit cap from this seed data. Flag unclear advance-rent/deposit handling.

rules.cap_formula: Security deposit, surety bond, or combination may not exceed three months' rent.
rules.product_risk_note: NV deposit pressure can be high but has a statutory upper bound.

rules.cap_formula: Landlord may not demand or receive a security deposit over two months' periodic rent.
rules.product_risk_note: VA risk checker can flag required deposits over 2x periodic rent.

rules.cap_formula: Seed source governs written agreement, checklist, statement, documentation, and 30-day return duties; this dataset does not assert a statewide amount cap.
rules.product_risk_note: Do not claim a WA deposit cap from this seed data. Ask whether written checklist and deposit terms are provided.

rules.cap_formula: Seed source governs security deposits and prepaid rent, including written agreement listing and return/accounting duties; this dataset does not assert a statewide amount cap.
rules.product_risk_note: For OR, focus on written deposit/prepaid-rent listing and return/accounting duties unless a local rule is added.

rules.cap_formula: Seed source governs return of security deposit and does not assert a statewide amount cap in this dataset.
rules.product_risk_note: CO return deadline can extend by lease up to 60 days; do not claim a deposit cap from this seed data.
